title: The code of  Lattice Boltzmann Simulations for Geometric and Topological Analysis of Soil Pores Effectively Predicts Multiscale Hydraulic Responses Under Freeze-Thaw Cycles
author: Wang, Fan
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This repository contains the  Python implementation of a D3Q19 lattice Boltzmann method (LBM) solver  used in the manuscript  “Geometric and Topological Analysis of Soil Pores Effectively Predicts Multiscale Hydraulic Responses Under Freeze-Thaw Cycles: Evidence from CT Scanning and Lattice Boltzmann Simulations”  for pore-scale flow simulations on voxelized porous media derived from X-ray CT images. The code is intended to support  reproducible  permeability and flow-field calculations in complex soil/rock pore networks, consistent with the modeling workflow reported in the paper. 
 Main capabilities 
 
 
  D3Q19 LBM  for single-phase incompressible flow in 3D voxel domains 
 
 
 Direct simulation on  binary pore/solid geometries  (e.g., CT-segmented volumes and REV subcubes) . User-configurable boundary conditions and driving scheme (as described in the manuscript) 
 
 
 Exports simulation outputs (e.g., velocity components, pressure/derived fields, masks, and/or summary maps) as  TIFF stacks  for downstream visualization and quantitative analysis 
 
 
 Postprocessing utilities to compute bulk hydraulic metrics (e.g., discharge/flux and permeability) from the simulated fields (if included) 
 
 
 Repository structure 
 
 
  src/ : core D3Q19 LBM solver (Python) 
 
 
  examples/ : minimal runnable examples and parameter templates 
 
 
  io/  or  utils/ : TIFF I/O and preprocessing/postprocessing helpers 
 
 
  README.md : installation, usage instructions, and parameter definitions 
 
 
 Reproducing results from the paper 
 
 
 Prepare the voxel domain(s) following the CT processing/segmentation steps described in the manuscript. 
 
 
 Set simulation parameters (grid spacing, relaxation time, forcing/pressure gradient, iteration limit, convergence criterion) in the provided configuration/template file(s).Run the solver for each domain/REV. 
 
 
 Use the included postprocessing scripts to compute permeability/flux and generate the reported statistics and figures. Additional notes on recommended parameter ranges and convergence settings are provided in  README.md . 
 
 
 Requirements 
 Python  [version, e.g., ≥3.9]  with standard scientific packages (e.g., NumPy, SciPy, and TIFF I/O libraries).  
 License and citation 
 If you use this code, please cite: 
 
 
 the Zenodo record for this repository (10.5281/zenodo.18144448)
